name: SONIC — SPEED PROTOCOL: THE BLUE BLUR
fantasy: Gotta go fast. Build real, usable speed the way athletes actually do it — quick feet, explosive hops, clean sprint form, and the sense to warm up and recover so you never break down. The zones get faster; you get faster with them.
core_rule: Speed is a quality, not a grind — warm up first, go max, then fully recover.
core_rule_enforcement: Every stage starts with a MANDATORY ~15-min neuromuscular / dynamic warm-up — high-intensity speed work stays locked until it's done. Sprint and jump work is QUALITY-capped (foot-contact caps on plyometrics, low sprint volume with full recovery between reps) and never taken to exhaustion or through pain. You go fast when you're fresh; junk volume is not rewarded.
mechanic: SPEED GATE + QUALITY-CAPPED EFFORT. Six speed pillars — running/endurance, sprint/acceleration, agility/footwork, plyometrics, parkour fundamentals, and speed-focused bodyweight. Each session is gated behind a mandatory dynamic warm-up (the research shows it BOTH cuts injury AND improves sprint/jump performance). Max-effort work is then capped for quality: plyometric foot-contacts per session, sprint reps/distance, and enforced full recovery between reps. Stages are themed as Sonic zones with a rising 'speed' — motivational packaging only (see sourcing_note); the training variable that actually rises is skill and controlled volume, never recklessness.
sourcing_note: Grounded in the deep-research report (protocol-src/research/sonic-research-report.md — 105-agent multi-source deep research, adversarially verified, 24 confirmed / 1 refuted). What is REAL and used: the mandatory neuromuscular warm-up (FIFA 11+ style, ~15 min) reduces lower-extremity injury AND improves sprint/jump/balance; plyometric foot-contact caps (beginner ~60-80/session, sets of 5-10 reps, >=1 min rest, 48h between plyo sessions, 24h ok for low-amplitude), intensity-based progression (skips/squat jumps -> lateral/tuck jumps -> box/depth jumps), bilateral before unilateral, cut foot-contacts 50% on pain, soft bent-knee landings; low-volume/high-quality max-velocity sprints (40-60m x 3-4 reps, ~120-240m, full recovery); eccentric hamstring work (Nordic curls) cuts hamstring injury; ankle is the highest-risk parkour joint so ankle stability + safe landing/rolling come FIRST, master before advanced jumps; recovery rests on sleep/nutrition/hydration, not gadgets. FANTASY (flavor only, never trainable content): superhuman top speed and flips/tricks. REFUTED (not used as literal content): the '~100 landings before each height increment' scheme — the principle 'master landings before adding height' is sound, the exact rep-count is not evidence-based, so copy says 'master before progressing,' never a hard number. CONSERVATIVE CONVENTION (flagged, not from a verified source): the exact per-stage rep/distance/rest numbers, the ~10%/week running-volume ramp, agility-drill parameters, and the deload cadence — built from standard programming practice, since the verified research did not prescribe them.
fit_focus: Agility, footwork and change of direction

easy.label: JOG PACE
easy.rest: longest, full recovery between all hard efforts

normal.label: GOTTA GO FAST
normal.rest: moderate, but full recovery is preserved for max-effort reps

hard.label: SUPERSONIC
hard.rest: shorter between accessory work, but max-velocity sprints ALWAYS keep full recovery
hard.note: Beginner plyo cap is 60-80 foot-contacts (research). Hard sits at the top of that beginner band, NOT above it. Full recovery between max sprints is never traded away for density.

hard_never_requires: Plyometric foot-contacts above the beginner cap (80)
Cutting recovery short between max-velocity sprints
Flips, tricks, or advanced jumps before landing/rolling is mastered
Adding running volume faster than about 10% per week
Training a hard pillar two days in a row without recovery

speed_pillars.running: Aerobic base + interval running, conversational vs hard pace, volume ramped gradually.
speed_pillars.sprint: Acceleration (short 10-20m starts, mechanics) and max-velocity (40-60m, full recovery).
speed_pillars.agility: Ladder, cone drills (5-10-5 pro-agility, T-drill), short shuttles, reactive change of direction.
speed_pillars.plyometrics: Intensity-progressed: skips/pogos/squat jumps -> lateral/tuck jumps/broad jumps -> box/low depth jumps. Foot-contact capped.
speed_pillars.parkour: Landing, safety roll, precision jump (low), two-handed vault, wall climb-up. Ankle-first, no flips.
speed_pillars.bodyweight: Speed-supporting strength — single-leg work, calf/ankle, eccentric hamstring (Nordic curls), core.

safety_scaffolding.why: Speed training's real risks are overuse (shin splints, Achilles/patellar tendinopathy, stress fractures) and acute injury (ankle sprains, hamstring strains). The research shows both training-load spikes AND lack of conditioning independently raise injury risk, the ankle is the highest-risk parkour joint, and a structured warm-up meaningfully lowers injury while improving performance.
rules: MANDATORY WARM-UP GATE: every session begins with a ~15-min neuromuscular / dynamic warm-up (joint mobility, low-intensity movement, progressive build). It is not optional and high-intensity work is locked until it is complete — it both reduces injury and improves sprint/jump performance.

PLYO FOOT-CONTACT CAPS: beginner cap ~60-80 foot-contacts per session, in sets of 5-10 reps with >=1 min rest, at least 48h between plyometric sessions (24h acceptable only for low-amplitude pogo/ankle/line hops). Progress by intensity, bilateral before unilateral. If pain occurs, cut total foot-contacts by 50% and land soft with bent knees.
SPRINT QUALITY, FULL RECOVERY: max-velocity work is low-volume/high-quality (40-60m x 3-4 reps, ~120-240m total) with FULL recovery between reps. Never sprint through fatigue or pain.
GRADUAL RUN-VOLUME RAMP: increase running volume gradually (about 10% per week as a conservative convention) with a built-in deload (Stage 11) before the finale.

PARKOUR ANKLE-FIRST: learn safe landing (absorb with bent knees, never 'stick' a stiff landing) and the safety roll BEFORE any jumps; ankle stability is the top priority. Start low with cleared landings, master before progressing height, and NEVER attempt flips or tricks.
CONDITION THE VULNERABLE LINKS: include eccentric hamstring work (Nordic curls) and ankle/calf work — lack of conditioning is an independent injury factor.
RECOVERY = FUNDAMENTALS: recovery leans on sleep, nutrition, and hydration, not gadgets or modalities.
user_facing_stop_criteria: Sharp or localized joint pain — especially ankle, knee, or shin
Shin pain that builds during running (possible shin splints / stress reaction)
Sudden sharp pain in the back of the thigh (possible hamstring strain)
Dizziness, lightheadedness, chest pain, or shortness of breath
safety_scaffolding.stop_action: Any stop criterion halts the session immediately with a plain-language caution (stop, ice/rest; sharp joint or tendon pain that persists = seek medical advice) and applies no XP penalty.
safety_scaffolding.screening_note: Beginners and anyone returning from a lower-limb injury should start at JOG PACE, keep to the low end of every cap, and add volume slowly — surface this on first entry.

equipment_profiles.rule: Fully doable with open space + a timer. A ~60m run space (park path, quiet street, track, or treadmill) unlocks the outdoor sprint variants; ladder/cones/low-step enrich agility, plyo, and parkour drills but every stage has a minimum-viable route (floor lines, on-the-spot shuttles). No heavy equipment.

achievements.name: Warm-Up Complete
achievements.trigger: Complete the mandatory warm-up gate for the first time (Stage 1)

achievements.name: Free Runner
achievements.trigger: Complete Stage 9 (parkour fundamentals) — landings mastered, no flips

achievements.name: Never Skip the Warm-Up
achievements.trigger: Complete a full stage with the warm-up gate done and zero pain flags

achievements.name: Rest Is Speed
achievements.trigger: Complete the Stage 11 deload / recovery zone

causes: Form breaks down (sloppy landings, reaching sprints)
Effort taken to exhaustion instead of stopping at the cap
response: Re-run the warm-up before any speed work
Reduce reps/distance or foot-contacts, keep the quality high
Enforce full recovery — never chase junk volume
Quality over quantity: speed is only trained when fresh

safety_stop: Dizziness / chest pain / shortness of breath

final_stage.name: Final Zone — The Blue Blur
final_stage.rule: The finale — a quality speed gauntlet: full warm-up gate, then acceleration -> one capped max-velocity sprint set (full recovery) -> an agility circuit -> a small capped plyo finisher. Hit clean, fast reps and STOP at the cap. Never to exhaustion or failure. Enter rested (Stage 11 was the deload).
final_stage.outcome: FINAL CLEAR -> victory screen + The Blue Blur / Sonic Speed achievements.

art.art_note: Placeholder — a blue-blur / speed-lines / Green-Hill-inspired banner would suit; swap before launch. Keep original theming (avoid trademarked Sonic character art).
